TASK CREATION RULES – PARAMETERS, VOLUME PAGEREF _Toc97624979 \h 12 5.1 VOLUME PARAMETER PAGEREF _Toc97624980 \h 12 1 . GLOBAL EXTENSION CONTROL Code Setup Enablement System Code Navigate to the System Codes (Configuration) UI. If system code C- 000 ( Global Extens ion Control ) does not yet exist, click Add. Populate the followin g information: 232410 174433 Note: The checkbox “System code parameters exist” will auto populate upon completion of subsequent steps for adding parameters, and it is un - editable to the user. Click Save. Next, click the Code ID’s Action Button The System Code ID’s screen is displayed. right 353276 0 0 Click ADD. Populate the following information provided above. Click SAVE to save the Code ID. Next, Click the Parameters Action button located on the bottom right of the screen to Navigate back to System Code Parameters screen. System Code Parameters Screen is displayed. Click ADD button. 344805 151933 Populate the following information provided above. Click SAVE. Click on the Parameters tab 189290 417782 C heck the box to enable all custom functionality. Click SAVE. 2. ADD C-0 0 0-EX 2 9 CODE ID TO C- 000 G LO BAL CODE add C-000-EX29 138023 631981 Navigate to System Codes UI in WM. We will need to add Custom code C- 000 - EX 2 9 if it has not been added. Search for system code C - 000 : Use the Custom Code drop-down option under the Record Type menu. Type in 000 for Code Type. Click apply to search. Click the Checkbox to select the system code. Click on the Code ID's action button in the lower right of the menu. Click ADD action button. Populate the fields with the information provided in the screenshot above . Click SAVE. C- 000 - EX 2 9 Enablement Click the Blue Parameters Tab. Located next to the DETAILS tab. Check the box to enable all custom functionality. CLICK SAVE. 3 . ADD PICK LOCATION DETERMINATION ZONE CUSTOM BYTE 3.1 Add system code B- 331 BYTE 9 7 -9 7 , EX29 Hard location maximum Navigate to System Codes UI, filter for System Code Type 331 Click the Checkbox to select the system code. Click on the Parameters action button in the lower right of the menu. Click ADD action button Populate the fields with the information provided in the screenshot above . Click Save . Navigate back to system code 331 . Click the Checkbox to select the system code. Click on the Code IDs action button in the lower right of the menu. Select the Checkbox for the Pick Determination Zones that will be enabled for EX2 9 . Click the blue Parameters tab. Check the box for parameter 9 7 -9 7 to enable custom functionality for EX2 9 . Click Save. 4 . ADD C- E29-E29 FOR DEFAULT PICK LOCATION MAX 4 .1 Enablement System Code Navigate to System Codes UI, Click add Exact text descriptions are in extension spec. Otherwise fill in as below. Save Reload to System Codes page. Select C-E29, select Parameters. Input below information. Save. Select Code IDs, select Add. Input below information. Parameters tab, input below. VALUE HERE IS DETERMINED BY McLANE team. Save. 5 . TASK CREATION RULES – PARAMETERS, VOLUME 5.1 VOLUME PARAMETER To break the Allocation Inventory Detail across different tasks, we need to ensure that we are breaking by the volume parameter. As this extension makes no changes to the base Task Creation Rules, we still can combine these into one task if we need to. For the areas where we do not want them to combine, the below configuration will ensure that ALL P50 AID records end up on INDIVIDUAL TASKS for this Task Creation Rule. Adding the above maximum volume constraint will ensure that each allocation record is broken across individual tasks. This extension only splits AID records for INT-9 replenishment at the Unit Level for P50 allocations. You can use this behavior only in instances where that is the case. View the spec for further details.
